The '''Arrow-17 airspeeder''' was a product of [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech Repulsor Company]].

==Description==
The Arrow-17 airspeeder was manufactured and sold by [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech Repulsor Company]]. It required one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] to operate, and had room for three passengers. The back of the [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] contained enough room to mount a rack of [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]].<ref name="FC" />

==History==
Built by Aratech Repulsor Company, the Arrow-17 was active during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Gamgalon]] owned three Arrow-17 airspeeders that he used to ferry safari hunters from the settlement of [[Tropis-on-Varonat]] to his safari base camp in the [[Great Jungle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]].<ref name="FC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Arrow-17 airspeeder first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[First Contact]]'', which was published in [[1994]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] by [[West End Games]]. ''First Contact'' was subsequently reprinted in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]''. ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'', and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']].
